verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific single-pass transmembrane protein (Ig-like domain architecture) essential for sperm-egg plasma membrane fusion. Its extracellular domain is exposed on the outer leaflet of the sperm plasma membrane and binds its egg-side receptor JUNO (FOLR4) to mediate gamete fusion. However, expression is strictly restricted to mature sperm cells — a narrow germline/reproductive lineage. Per the triage rules, surface-accessible proteins with their own TM anchor whose display is restricted to germline/gamete lineages map to 'contextual'/'tissue_restricted_surface' rather than 'yes'. The protein body is unambiguously on the extracellular face of the sperm PM via its own single-pass TM mechanism, but only in that highly restricted cell type.
